title: array slice not working properly?
body:
Not sure if it's a misunderstanding on my part but looked at both 1800-2017 and some online examples:

Verilator 5.017 devel rev v5.014-145-g44e7d2ebe

verilator -sv  --cc --timing --binary -Wno-INITIALDLY --trace-depth 0 --top add_tb --timescale 1ns/100ps add_tb.sv
%Warning-WIDTHEXPAND: add_tb.sv:121:15: Operator ASSIGN expects 256 bits on the Assign RHS, but Assign RHS's SLICESEL generates 4 bits.
                                      : ... note: In instance 'add_tb.test'
  121 |             a = act.vector[127:64];
      |               ^

a is declared as an output in a program #(W=4)

    output logic [64*W-1:0]       a,


class Activation;
    rand logic signed [3:0] vector[127:0]; // Dynamic array of 4-bit logic vectors

    Activation act = new;

So act.vector[127:64] should return 64 elements of 4 bits, no? Why does it say "Assign RHS's SLICESEL generates 4 bits." ?
